title: Projects
text:
Flagship Research Programme


This flagship research programme examines elite scientists’ career mobility across institutions and cities, and the relations among knowledge connections, collaboration networks, research platforms, and urban innovation conditions. It links individual career trajectories with urban-scale network structures using longitudinal data on biographies, publications, institutions, and cities.

title: Projects
text:
Selected Research Projects


Collaborative Research Project

Daily Nighttime Lights and Urban Resilience Trajectories
Research Question. How can high-frequency nighttime light data be used to identify changes and recovery trajectories in urban activity after short-term external shocks?
Data. VIIRS-DNB daily nighttime light data for 48 cities in the Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei, Yangtze River Delta, and Pearl River Delta urban agglomerations.
Methods. Nighttime-light remote sensing processing, Fisher Information, dynamic time warping, and K-means clustering.
Role. The available public materials support a cautious description of participation in data processing, resilience-proxy construction, trajectory comparison, and clustering analysis within a collaborative research setting.
Status. Collaborative Research Project.



Ongoing Research

Talent Mobility and Biomedical Innovation Systems in China and the United States
Research Question. How do top scientist training and career mobility patterns correspond to structural differences in biomedical innovation systems in China and the United States?
Data. Biographical, publication, patent, institutional, city-level, and industry-related data on top biomedical scientists in China and the United States.
Methods. Biographical analysis, multi-source data matching, social network analysis, and comparative research.
Role. Based on public information, the work is described cautiously as data organization, biography matching, network construction, and comparative analysis support.
Status. Ongoing Research.

title: Projects
text:
Earlier Research


Earlier Research

Global Trade Network Evolution and Sectoral Differences under the COVID-19 Shock
Research Question. What structural changes appeared in the global commodity trade network and in different product-type networks under the COVID-19 shock?
Data. 2019-2021 United Nations commodity trade data covering 219 countries and regions.
Methods. Directed weighted networks, social network analysis, product-type comparison, and spatial analysis.
Role. Public information supports a cautious description of data organization, trade network construction, network indicator calculation, and spatial interpretation.
Status. Earlier Research.

title: Research
text:
My research is grounded in economic geography, urban and regional economics, and science of science. The core question is how scientific careers, knowledge connections, institutions, and cities are organized across space, and how these relations are associated with urban and regional innovation.
The main empirical setting is elite biomedical scientists’ mobility across institutions and cities. Related projects on urban resilience, China-U.S. biomedical innovation systems, and global trade networks extend the same training in relational data, longitudinal comparison, and spatial network analysis.

title: Research
text:
My research is grounded in economic geography, urban and regional economics, and science of science. The core question is how scientific careers, knowledge connections, institutions, and cities are organized across space, and how these relations are associated with urban and regional innovation.
The main empirical setting is elite biomedical scientists’ mobility across institutions and cities. Related projects on urban resilience, China-U.S. biomedical innovation systems, and global trade networks extend the same training in relational data, longitudinal comparison, and spatial network analysis.

title: Research
text:
Core Research Directions


Scientific Mobility and Career Trajectories
I examine how scientists move across institutions and cities, how individual career histories can be represented as longitudinal records, and how destination choice can be studied with event-level candidate sets.


Urban and Regional Innovation
This line connects scientist mobility, institutional platforms, collaboration networks, and city-level opportunities to observed differences in urban and regional innovation systems.


Scientific Knowledge Networks and Relational Data
I build and audit linked scientist-institution-publication-city data, keeping affiliations, publication locations, collaboration ties, and mobility events analytically distinct.


Urban Resilience and High-Frequency Spatial Data
Work on daily nighttime lights extends the methodological toolkit to high-frequency spatial data, time-series trajectories, and city-cluster comparison.

title: Research
text:
Data and Methods


Longitudinal multi-source data construction
Scientist-institution-city linkage and entity resolution
Social network analysis and ego-network analysis
Conditional Logit models with event-level candidate destinations
ERGM/WERGM specifications for network analysis
GIS, ArcGIS, Python, R, Stata, NetworkX, Gephi, and Ucinet

title: Research
text:
Current Research Agenda


Scientific Mobility, Knowledge Networks, and Urban Innovation
This flagship programme studies top scientists’ career mobility across institutions and cities, and the relations among knowledge-space connections, collaboration networks, research platforms, and urban innovation conditions.


Destination Choice among Elite Biomedical Scientists
The working paper focuses on whether pre-move publication-location exposure is associated with subsequent destination choice among candidate cities in elite biomedical scientist mobility events.


Comparative and Methodological Extensions
China-U.S. biomedical innovation comparison, daily nighttime-light resilience analysis, and earlier global trade network research broaden the empirical domains while retaining the focus on spatial organization, networks, and longitudinal evidence.

title: Ran Wang
text: Ran Wang

title: Ran Wang
text:
Research Positioning
My work connects individual scientific careers with the urban and regional systems in which knowledge production takes place. The main line of research examines how elite scientists move across institutions and cities, how prior knowledge-space connections are associated with destination choice, and how mobility and collaboration networks relate to urban innovation conditions.

title: Ran Wang
text:
Research Themes


Scientific Mobility and Career Trajectories
Longitudinal records of scientists, institutions, publications, and cities are used to describe career moves and intercity mobility networks.


Urban and Regional Innovation
The research links mobility, institutional platforms, collaboration networks, and city-level opportunities without treating observed associations as causal claims beyond the design.


Relational Data and Spatial Methods
Author-institution-city linkage, geocoding checks, network indicators, and event-level candidate sets support reproducible empirical work.

title: Publications
text:
Accepted Article



城市网络视角下全球生物医药顶尖科学家流动的空间结构与影响因素

This accepted article examines the spatial structure and related factors of top global biomedical scientists’ mobility from an urban network perspective. The page records its accepted status without adding unconfirmed journal metadata, page ranges, or formal publication-year information.

title: Publications
text:
Working Paper

Working Paper

Pre-move Publication-Location Exposure and Destination Choice among Elite Biomedical Scientists

知识空间联系与全球顶尖科学家的流动网络研究

This working paper studies how pre-move publication-location exposure is associated with later city mobility and destination choice among elite biomedical scientists, using event-level candidate destination sets and Conditional Logit models.

title: Publications
text:
Master’s Thesis

Master’s Thesis

Intercity Mobility Network of Top Global Biomedical Scientists: A Dual Perspective of Whole and Ego Networks

全球生物医药顶尖科学家城际流动网络——基于全域与自我网络双重视角

The master’s thesis analyzes the intercity mobility network of top global biomedical scientists from whole-network and ego-network perspectives, linking individual career trajectories with city-level network structure.
